# a0p — research instrument

> Donation-funded BYOK multi-model AI workspace + a0(ZFAE) native inference engine on the prime-tensor stack (PCNA → PCTA → PTCA → ZFAE), with the fiq motion canon as the boundary law for all inter-stratum motion. PCEA seals every state transition.

## What this is

Not a chat clone. a0p is a research instrument that lets the user:

- **Bring their own keys** to OpenAI / Anthropic / Google / xAI; never depends on any platform-specific runtime.
- **Run native a0(ZFAE) inference**. The ZFAE engine has a 407,729-scalar weight bank (`zfae_core.safetensors`), trains by teacher distillation, and refuses to fall back to any LLM in `zfae_native` mode.
- **Compare frontier models** side by side (fan-out / daisy-chain / synthesis) when explicitly invoked as a *comparison tool*, not as the agent's voice.
- **Inspect the substrate**: live tensor / circle / seed / core layers; six-ring topology (Φ Ψ Ω Θ Σ MemL MemS); fiq motion events with `χ_route · χ_audit · χ_support · χ_attention` indicators; PCEA-encrypted state across heartbeats.
- **Maintain persistent agents** as semi-permanent character-sheet-bound instances (full CRUD), each with its own ring state + memory + per-instance ZFAE checkpoint.

## Layered prime-tensor stack

```
PCNA  (tensors + backprop)     →  weights ┐
PCTA  (circles → seeds)       ─┐          ├→ a0(ZFAE) inference → output
PTCA  (seeds → core)          ─┴→ motion ─┘
PCEA  (orthogonal guardian — seals weights/state; not a stack layer)
```

Single source of truth for layer roles: `The-Interdependency/interdependent-lib/docs/prime-tensor-stack.md`.

## Skill compliance

Every Python module under `/app/backend` declares its `MODULE_BUILD`, `BOUNDARIES`, `CAPABILITIES` (and where applicable `DOCS`, `CONTRACTS`) blocks. Composition ratios are bookended via `# === RATIOS ===` at the head and tail of every file. Eleven canonical skills from `skill-lib` are vendored at `/app/.agents/skills/<skill>/`:

| Skill | Block | Runner |
|---|---|---|
| `msdmd` | foundational | `python -m a0p_skills.module_build_runner /app/backend` |
| `meta-module-build` | `MODULE_BUILD` | `a0p_skills.module_build_runner` |
| `risk-boundary-build` | `BOUNDARIES` | `a0p_skills.boundaries_runner` |
| `cap-build` | `CAPABILITIES` | `a0p_skills.capabilities_runner` |
| `doc-build` | `DOCS` | `a0p_skills.docs_runner` |
| `test-build` | `CONTRACTS` | `a0p_skills.test_build_runner` |
| `ratios` | `RATIOS` (bookend) | `a0p_skills.ratios_runner` |
| `manifest` | splice `<!-- BEGIN/END GENERATED:manifest -->` | `python .agents/skills/manifest/generate.py --check` |
| `canon` | procedural | doctrine for source-backed vs proposed vs `hmmm` |
| `visitor-intro` | procedural | onboarding tour for new arrivals |
| `char-compress` | procedural | flesh/bone discipline for SKILL.md authorship + agent-archive compression |

## What's known but unresolved

Open boundaries live in `/app/HMMM.md` and `/app/REBUILD_PLAN.md`. Per the `canon` skill, `hmmm` is an explicit boundary object, not a missing-fact.

## How to run

```bash
sudo supervisorctl restart backend     # FastAPI on :8001 (proxied via /api)
sudo supervisorctl restart frontend    # CRA dev on :3000

python -m a0p_skills.module_build_runner /app/backend
python -m a0p_skills.test_build_runner   /app/backend
python .agents/skills/manifest/generate.py --root /app --pyproject backend/pyproject.toml --check
```
